title: buildah does not respect BUILDAH_ISOLATION for run command
body:
**Description**

The `buildah run` command ignores the BUILDAH_ISOLATION flag. This causes it to fail in ubuntu in a rootless environment (related to #1498).

This is expected to be related to how the run command handles setting the isolation option in `cmd/buildah/run.go`:

```go
func runCmd(...) {
    ...

    isolation := builder.Isolation
    if c.Flag("isolation").Changed {
        isolation, err = parse.IsolationOption(c)
        if err != nil {
            return err
        }
    }

    ...
}
```

Because the `builder.Isolation` differs from ubuntu (set to `IsolationOCI`) and fedora/OpenSuse/centos (set to `IsolationOCIRootless`), the command fails in ubuntu. 

Also, the flag gets parsed correctly if we set `--isolation` flag with empty string. For example, in OpenSuse (works by default):

```
$ BUILDAH_ISOLATION=rootless podman run --isolation= $container echo hello
** Succeeds **
$ BUILDAH_ISOLATION=oci podman run --isolation= $container echo hello
** Fails **
```

Note: the `buildah bud` command parses the flag as expected, so the inner run commands succeed.

**Solution**

Maybe change the code to always get the isolation from `parse.IsolationOption(c)` like:

```go
isolation, err := parse.IsolationOption(c)
if err != nil {
    return err
}
```
 
**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. `container=$(podman from fedora)`
2. `podman run $(container) echo hello` (on ubuntu)

**Describe the results you received:**

```
container_linux.go:344: starting container process caused "process_linux.go:424: container init caused \"rootfs_linux.go:58: mounting \\\"sysfs\\\" to rootfs \\\"/tmp/buildah060906010/mnt/rootfs\\\" at \\\"/sys\\\" caused \\\"operation not permitted\\\"\""
error running container: error creating container for [/bin/echo dddd]: : exit status 1
error while running runtime: exit status 1
ERRO[0000] exit status 1
```

This is because the isolation is set to `IsolationOCI`, instead of `IsolationOCIRootless`

**Describe the results you expected:**

Running the command successfully.

**Output of `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah/bionic,now 1.8-1~dev~ubuntu18.04~ppa25 amd64 [installed]
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
buildah version 1.8-dev (image-spec 1.0.0, runtime-spec 1.0.0)
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

(ubuntu bionic 18.04.2)

```
DISTRIB_ID=Ubuntu
DISTRIB_RELEASE=18.04
DISTRIB_CODENAME=bionic
DISTRIB_DESCRIPTION="Ubuntu 18.04.2 LTS"
NAME="Ubuntu"
VERSION="18.04.2 LTS (Bionic Beaver)"
ID=ubuntu
ID_LIKE=debian
PRETTY_NAME="Ubuntu 18.04.2 LTS"
VERSION_ID="18.04"
HOME_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/"
SUPPORT_URL="https://help.ubuntu.com/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/"
PRIVACY_POLICY_URL="https://www.ubuntu.com/legal/terms-and-policies/privacy-policy"
VERSION_CODENAME=bionic
UBUNTU_CODENAME=bionic
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux test-vm 4.15.0-48-generic #51-Ubuntu SMP Wed Apr 3 08:28:49 UTC 2019 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

**Output of `cat /etc/containers/storage.conf`:**

(default)

```
# storage.conf is the configuration file for all tools
# that share the containers/storage libraries
# See man 5 containers-storage.conf for more information

# The "container storage" table contains all of the server options.
[storage]

# Default Storage Driver
driver = "vfs"

# Temporary storage location
runroot = "/var/run/containers/storage"

# Primary read-write location of container storage
graphroot = "/var/lib/containers/storage"

[storage.options]
# AdditionalImageStores is used to pass paths to additional read-only image stores
# Must be comma separated list.
additionalimagestores = [
]

# Size is used to set a maximum size of the container image.  Only supported by
# certain container storage drivers (currently overlay, zfs, vfs, btrfs)
size = ""

# OverrideKernelCheck tells the driver to ignore kernel checks based on kernel version
override_kernel_check = "true"
```

comments.body: @bernardomig Thanks for the issue and heads up.  We'll take a look at it.
